intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good — I see three 10x samples here. You asked for GSM5746268 specifically. Now let me read the Seurat workflow recipe to build an accurate plan:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Perfect. Now let me present the plan with sensible defaults for a standard PBMC workflow:

input.title: Seurat scRNA-seq workflow: GSM5746268 QC → clustering → markers
input.summary: Standard Seurat v5 processing on one 10x PBMC sample: load, QC filter, normalize, HVG, PCA, Leiden clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; visualize with UMAP plot and top markers table per cluster.
input.rationale: You'll get a complete, publication-ready scRNA-seq analysis with cluster assignment and marker genes in one pass.
assumptions: Data is a standard 10x mtx triplet (barcodes/features/matrix) for one sample (GSM5746268_*)
Mitochondrial genes are prefixed 'MT-' (will confirm from data)
QC thresholds: nFeature_RNA 200–5000, percent.mt < 10% (standard PBMC defaults)
Use 30 PCs for the analysis (read from elbow plot if you'd prefer a different number)
Leiden clustering at resolution=0.5 (adjust if you want finer/coarser; will show the clustering tree)

steps.title: Load 10x mtx triplet and create Seurat object
steps.description: Read barcodes.tsv, features.tsv, and matrix.mtx.gz; create object with min.cells=3, min.features=200 defaults
expected_outputs: Raw count object with cell and gene counts

steps.title: QC: compute metrics and visualize before filtering
steps.description: Add nFeature_RNA, nCount_RNA, percent.mt; generate pre-filter QC plots (violin plots, scatter)

steps.title: Subset on QC thresholds and show post-filter stats
steps.description: Filter to nFeature_RNA ∈ [200, 5000], percent.mt < 10%; report cells retained

steps.title: Normalize, find variable features, scale
expected_outputs: Processed object with normalized slot and variable features

steps.title: PCA and elbow plot
steps.description: RunPCA → ElbowPlot to guide PC selection; default to 30 PCs

steps.title: Neighbors, clustering (Leiden), and UMAP
steps.description: FindNeighbors → FindClusters (res=0.5) → RunUMAP on first 30 PCs

steps.title: UMAP visualization
steps.description: DimPlot of UMAP colored by cluster

steps.title: FindAllMarkers and top-markers table
steps.description: Identify DE genes per cluster (Wilcox test, min.pct=0.25, logfc.threshold=0.25); extract top 5 per cluster
